front: Composer: student of John Blow; succeeded him as principal organist at Westminster Abbey; modeled the final chorus of Dido and Aeneas on Blow's Venus and Adonis
Composer: works catalogued by Z numbers compiled by musicologist Franklin Zimmerman; posthumous song collection called Orpheus Britannicus
Composer: incidental music for Aphra Behn's Abdelazer provided the rondeau theme Benjamin Britten used in The Young Person's Guide to the Orchestra
Composer: wrote music for the funeral of Queen Mary II — a C minor March for four flatt (slide) trumpets, a Canzona, and a setting of 'Thou Knowest Lord'
Composer: birthday ode 'Come, Ye Sons of Art' (for Queen Mary II) features the countertenor duet 'Sound the Trumpet'; its opening sinfonia was recycled in The Indian Queen
Composer: Ode to St. Cecilia ('Hail! Bright Cecilia') features the movement 'Wondrous Machine!' in praise of the organ
Composer: attributed as the author of the Irish march Lilliburlero
Opera: the dying queen sings 'Remember me, but ah! Forget my fate' over a chromatic descending ground bass (G to D); the lament is preceded by the recitative 'Thy hand, Belinda'
back: Dido's Lament ('When I am laid in earth') — Dido and Aeneas (Henry Purcell)
work: Dido and Aeneas – Dido's Lament ('When I am laid in earth')

front: Opera: the ground bass (chromatic descending fourth, G to D) has an unusual 5-bar repetition cycle instead of the more common 4
back: Dido's Lament — Dido and Aeneas (Henry Purcell)
work: Dido and Aeneas – Dido's Lament ('When I am laid in earth')

front: Opera: libretto by Nahum Tate; an evil sorceress tricks the Trojan hero into abandoning the Queen of Carthage; includes a chorus of witches and a chorus of sailors
Opera: libretto by John Dryden; Arthur rescues the blind princess Emmeline from the Saxon King Oswald; 'Frost Scene' features Cupid singing 'What Power art thou' over a three-bar C minor ground bass
Opera: adapted from A Midsummer Night's Dream; adds the Drunken Poet character; includes Juno singing 'Thrice Happy Lovers' and the Chinese Woman's aria 'Hark! Now the Echoing Air'
Composer: countertenor song 'Music for a While' from incidental music for an Oedipus-based play; published posthumously in Orpheus Britannicus
Composer: semi-opera Dioclesian is subtitled 'The Prophetess' and named for a Roman emperor; The Indian Queen was an unfinished dramatic opera set in Mexico
